business.headline: Reliance shares gain over 2%: Why is RIL stock rising today?Reliance Industries shares rose after Jio Platforms filed its draft papers for an IPO. The move sharpened focus on value unlocking and the group's next growth engines.

Can Jio become India's biggest IPO ever? Here's how it compares to top 5 IPOsIf current estimates hold true, the JIO IPO could raise as much as $4 billion, or around Rs 37,000 crore. That would comfortably surpass Hyundai Motor India's Rs 27,859 crore IPO, which currently holds the record as the largest public issue in Indian history.

NSE vs Jio IPO: Size, valuation, and profits. How do the two giants compare?NSE has filed its DRHP, while Jio Platforms is set to file its prospectus with Sebi. The twin moves bring India's most-awaited IPOs closer and spotlight financialisation and digitalisation.

Sensex opens 300 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; IT stocks gainThe S&P BSE Sensex gained 404.20 points, or 0.53%, to 77,207.10 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 rose 117.25 points, or 0.49%, to 24,130.35.

The taxman is using AI. These ITR mistakes could get flaggedTax experts have listed eight common ITR filing mistakes that can draw the department's attention. With AI-led data matching expanding, even small mismatches can delay refunds or trigger notices.

Why RBI drew the line on mis-selling by banksThe RBI has issued its strongest framework yet against mis-selling by banks and regulated entities. The regulations target dark patterns, forced bundling and unsuitable sales while shifting accountability to institutions.

Mukesh Ambani's Jio IPO is here: Reliance to offer up to 27 crore fresh sharesMukesh Ambani announced at Reliance's 49th AGM that Jio Platforms will file its DRHP with Sebi today. The move formally starts the long-awaited listing process and adds momentum to India's pipeline of mega public issues.

Sensex ends 600 points lower, Nifty tops 24,000; IT stocks bleedThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 607.08 points, or 0.78%, to close at 76,802.90, while the NSE Nifty50 shed 155.05 points, or 0.64%, to settle at 24,013.10.

Mukesh Ambani announces Jio IPO, draft papers to be filed with Sebi todayAddressing shareholders at Reliance Industries' 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM), Ambani said Jio Platforms' draft red herring prospectus (DRHP) has been approved by the company's board and will be filed with market regulator Sebi later today.

Government grants copyright society status to PPL India, boosting royaltiesThe government has registered PPL India as a Copyright Society under the Copyright Act, 1957. The move gives music users a clearer licensing route and is expected to improve royalty payments to rights holders and artists.

Why Infosys took the biggest hit after Accenture's warningAccenture reported strong AI demand but trimmed its growth outlook, signalling that clients are still holding back on spending. The warning hammered Indian IT stocks, with Infosys leading the rout.

Jio IPO moves ahead as Reliance bets big on AI and new energyRIL AGM 2026: Jio Platforms to file IPO papers with Sebi; Mukesh Ambani unveils five major value creation pathways, bets big on AI, energy self-reliance, satellite connectivity, media, FMCG and global expansion

Sensex down 700 points: Why is stock market falling today after a 5-day rally?The S&P BSE Sensex was down 635.85 points, or 0.84%, at 76,774.13, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 170.10 points, or 0.70%, to 23,997.90 as of 10:13 am. The decline snapped a strong rally that had seen the Sensex gain nearly 4.8% and the Nifty rise over 4.3% in the previous five sessions.

Reliance AGM: Will Mukesh Ambani finally reveal the Jio IPO date today?Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani is set to address shareholders at the company's 49th Annual General Meeting (AGM) today, and the biggest question on Dalal Street is simple: Will he finally announce a timeline for the Jio IPO?

Accenture warning sends Infosys, TCS and HCLTech tumbling. Here's whyMajor Indian IT stocks fell sharply after Accenture lowered the upper end of its annual revenue growth forecast. The sell-off has renewed concerns over weak discretionary spending, slower deal conversion and the pace of demand recovery.

Sensex, Nifty tumble as Accenture warning triggers IT crash on Dalal StreetInfosys, TCS and other technology stocks tumbled after the global IT giant flagged slower growth, dragging the broader market lower despite falling oil prices.

India's Swiss bank funds fall 8%, while direct deposits jump 50%Swiss National Bank data showed total funds linked to Indian clients fell in 2025 even as direct customer deposits rose sharply. The figures underline that Indian-linked assets in Switzerland are not the same as black money estimates and reflect a more transparent banking regime.

Jio IPO, AI, green energy: 5 big questions Mukesh Ambani may answer at RIL AGMMukesh Ambani is set to face shareholder questions on Jio's IPO timeline, AI plans and green energy projects at Reliance's 49th AGM. With key deadlines nearing, investors are seeking proof that earlier commitments are moving into execution.

Got your Form 16? Check these 6 details before filing your ITRSince Form 16 forms the foundation of income reporting for salaried taxpayers, spending a few minutes verifying the information can save you a lot of trouble later.

The third dimension – after inspectors and employers, now the consultantsIn the eleventh part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines the role of labour consultants who, instead of ensuring compliance, sometimes help employers create the appearance of compliance while facilitating violations of labour laws.

entertainment.headline: A Vijay for every generation: How Thalapathy rebuilt himself into Jana Nayagan As Vijay turns 52, his 33-year screen journey is traced through three distinct fan eras. From the romantic hero of the 90s to the Chief Minister of today, Vijay didn't just age with his audience, he rebuilt himself for each one.

Pankaj Tripathi's brother attacked with axe in land dispute, accused arrested Actor Pankaj Tripathi's brother was allegedly attacked with an axe in Bihar's Gopalganj district following a land dispute. Police have arrested neighbour Rajesh Sah in connection with the attack.

Exclusive: House of the Dragon cast on Season 3's war and aftermath In an exclusive chat, the House of the Dragon cast discussed Season 3's civil war and its fallout. They said the season's biggest impact may come from the emotional aftermath as much as the battles.

The Voice of Hind Rajab: All about film on a Palestinian girl now showing in India The Voice of Hind Rajab, a film based on the true story of a Palestinian girl, has finally released in Indian theatres after months of certification delays. The movie, blending real emergency calls with dramatic storytelling, offers a powerful insight into the humanitarian crisis in Gaza.

House of Dragon 3 Ep. 1 X review: Explosive twists, epic battles, absolute cinema House of the Dragon Season 3 has finally arrived, and Episode 1 is already generating strong reactions online. While many viewers praised its epic battle sequences, others questioned key story decisions.

Nora Fatehi dances as Rakesh Bedi brings Jameel Jamali charm to Ya Baba Actor-performer Nora Fatehi's Ya Baba got an unexpected twist with Dhurandhar star Rakesh Bedi joining the music video. The actor brings back his viral Jameel Jamali charm in the festive track.

Dhurandhar makes Ranveer Singh a Rs 325 crore star, joins SRK-Rajinikanth Actor Ranveer Singh has reportedly earned Rs 325 crore from the Dhurandhar duology, making it the biggest payday of his career. The actor's profit-sharing deal is said to have placed him alongside high-earning stars such as Shah Rukh Khan and Rajinikanth.

Epstein Island joke on Samay Raina's Latent 2 leaves Alia awkward, upsets fans India's Got Latent Season 2 has returned, but its first episode has already sparked controversy. From backlash over an Epstein Island joke to complaints about the show's format, viewers have plenty to say.

Why Sushant Singh Rajput and Shekhar Kapur's unrealised Paani would matter today As Mumbai and Goa dry up, and Rajasthan sees water protests, Paani, Shekhar Kapur's dream project with Sushant Singh Rajput, seems like the most important film Bollywood never made.

Karan Aujla's backstage moments with Virat Kohli are pure bromance goals Pop star Karan Aujla gave fans a glimpse of his candid moments with cricketer Virat Kohli backstage at their Delhi event. Their photos, hugs and on-stage chemistry quickly went viral, with fans calling it the collab of the year.

Pro-Khalistan protester arrested after disrupting Diljit Dosanjh's US show. Watch Diljit Dosanjh's San Francisco concert witnessed a security breach after a pro-Khalistan demonstrator rushed onto the stage and was arrested. The disruption came amid recent threats linked to hardline groups, but the singer continued the show.

Maa Inti Bangaaram box office Day 3: Samantha's film records blockbuster Sunday Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaaram ended its opening weekend with a strong Sunday at the box office. The steady rise in occupancy underlined growing traction for women-led releases in Telugu.

Cocktail 2 box office Day 3: Kriti-Shahid's film ends weekend on a high note A strong Sunday helped actors Kriti Sanon, Rashmika Mandanna and Shahid Kapoor's Cocktail 2 maintain its momentum at the box office. Here's a look at the film's Day 3 collections and weekend performance.

House of the Dragon 3 Episode 1-2 review: Westeros finally remembers how to burnRelease Date:Mon Jun 22, 2026 The first two episodes finally bring the Dance of the Dragons to life with powerful performances and gripping political drama. House of the Dragon Season 3 delivers its strongest start yet.

Inside Anshula Kapoor's pre-wedding rituals with Shanaya, Arjun, Janhvi and Khushi Actor Shanaya Kapoor shared pictures of cousin Anshula Kapoor's pre-wedding rituals. The post brought several Kapoor family members together ahead of her wedding to Rohan Thakkar.

Internet melts as Zakir Khan's father closes his last show with sitar performance Comedian Zakir Khan's final Mumbai show on the Papa Yaar tour ended with his father, Ustad Ismail Khan, making a surprise appearance on stage. The Father's Day moment echoed the show's father-son theme and drew an emotional response from fans.

Technician dies on Bhansali's Love and War set; production house offers Rs 40 lakh A technician died after an electric shock on the set of Love and War at Mumbai's Filmcity. The incident has led FWICE (Federation of Western India Cine Employees) to seek higher compensation for his family and stricter on-set safety.

Ashish Chanchlani backs Samay Raina's India's Got Latent 2: Fought countless battles Comedian Ashish Chanchlani backed comedian Samay Raina after criticism that India's Got Latent Season 2 felt too filtered. Chanchalani highlighted that the show returned only after a year-long legal battle and difficult compromises.

Nayanthara cheers for Samantha as Maa Inti Bangaaram earns Rs 25 crore worldwide Actor Nayanthara congratulated actor Samantha Ruth Prabhu after Maa Inti Bangaaram scored a strong opening at the box office. The film has crossed Rs 25 crore mark globally.

general news.headline: Iran walked out, JD Vance watched, Pakistanis stunned as cameras kept rollingThe Iranian delegation eventually returned to the negotiating table. But by then, a brief walkout, a surprised Pakistani delegation and a watchful JD Vance had already become the defining image of the day.

Will go to war: Pakistan's Khawaja Asif threatens India over Indus Waters TreatyPakistan's Defence Minister said Islamabad could go to war if India threatens its access to Indus waters. The warning sharpens tensions over the suspended treaty as Pakistan raises alarm over water security.

A message to selectors? Sooryavanshi's post-match chat after 29-ball 94 goes viralIndia A vs Sri Lanka A: Following a record-shattering 29-ball 94 against Sri Lanka A, teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has sent a clear message to senior ODI selectors, reminding his followers of his extensive 50-over pedigree in a viral post-match interview.

Ram Mandir donation row: Probe team flags lapses in counting cash, CCTV checksThe SIT probing alleged theft and misappropriation of Ayodhya Ram Temple donations has reported lapses in cash handling, hiring and surveillance. The findings also widened scrutiny to valuables, Maha Kumbh donations, land purchases and procurement.

JD Vance's glowing praise for Asim Munir, and the timing is hard to ignoreJD Vance praised Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Field Marshal Asim Munir for facilitating US-Iran dialogue. The remarks have sharpened attention on Washington's attempt to balance its India reset with continued engagement with Pakistan's military leadership.

Monsoon disruption in Mumbai as heavy rain floods roads, slows trafficIntense rain lashed Mumbai on Monday, bringing lightning, thunderstorms and waterlogging across several areas. The spell followed the monsoon's arrival and prompted a yellow alert over more rain and gusty winds.

NSE vs Jio IPO: Size, valuation, and profits. How do the two giants compare?NSE has filed its DRHP, while Jio Platforms is set to file its prospectus with Sebi. The twin moves bring India's most-awaited IPOs closer and spotlight financialisation and digitalisation.

Operation Tiger is successful: Fadnavis after 2 rebel UBT MPs switch to Shinde SenaMaharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis said "Operation Tiger" had succeeded after two Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs confirmed they would join Eknath Shinde's Sena. The defections triggered a fresh attack from Sena UBT chief Uddhav Thackeray, who said there was only "one Shiv Sena".

Iran leave handwritten note in Los Angeles locker room and it has a plea for peaceFIFA World Cup: Iran's players left an emotional handwritten message in their SoFi Stadium locker room on Sunday, thanking Los Angeles for its hospitality and supporters for standing by the team during a challenging World Cup campaign.

Did Qatar PM snub JD Vance? Awkward moment at US-Iran talks sparks buzzAn awkward exchange involving Qatar's Prime Minister and JD Vance marked the opening of the US-Iran talks in Switzerland. The incident and Iran's early gestures underscored the difficult mood around the new negotiations.

No change after CBSE re-evaluation? Students can now see answer books in personCBSE has allowed Class 12 students with unchanged review results to inspect their answer books at regional offices. The move adds transparency to the verification and re-evaluation process as revised marksheets are uploaded on DigiLocker.

Pankaj Tripathi's brother attacked with axe in land dispute, accused arrestedActor Pankaj Tripathi's brother was allegedly attacked with an axe in Bihar's Gopalganj district following a land dispute. Police have arrested neighbour Rajesh Sah in connection with the attack.

A Vijay for every generation: How Thalapathy rebuilt himself into Jana NayaganAs Vijay turns 52, his 33-year screen journey is traced through three distinct fan eras. From the romantic hero of the 90s to the Chief Minister of today, Vijay didn't just age with his audience, he rebuilt himself for each one.

Driest June in 146 years as monsoon begins moving, rain deficit at 46%After a nearly two-week stall, southwest monsoon winds have begun pushing into Mumbai and south Gujarat. The delayed revival leaves India with a 46 per cent June rain deficit and growing pressure on sowing and water supplies.

Parents greet students with a big applause as they exit re-NEET exam centresA viral Instagram video showed parents clapping for students as they came out after the NEET re-examination. The gesture drew attention as NEET UG 2026 was held under intensified security after the earlier exam controversy.

2 killed after BMW hits divider, flips several times; body parts scattered on roadAccording to preliminary information, the luxury vehicle was allegedly being driven at a speed of 250 kmph when the incident occurred. The accident was so severe that body parts were later found scattered on the road.

Cancer served on a plate? How chemicals banned in Europe are killing IndiansCarcinogenic chemicals, which get Indian agricultural exports rejected in Europe, continue to be sprayed at Indian farms without checks even though they are banned abroad. Why does the government allow these chemicals to find a place on Indian farms and then reach plates?

Monsoon's tantrum: Why Maharashtra is dry, but Rajasthan's deserts are wetRajasthan has received above-normal June rain even as Maharashtra faces a sharp monsoon deficit. The contrast reflects a disrupted monsoon pattern driven by western disturbances and weak moisture flow.

Rare tornado spins over Thoothukudi, a sight Tamil Nadu almost never sees. WatchA rare tornado touched down over Thoothukudi in Tamil Nadu on June 21, a state that almost never sees one. Here is what a tornado is, how it forms and why these twisters stay so unusual in southern India.

All six rebel Sena UBT MPs to join Shinde's Shiv Sena at 3 pmIn a major blow to Uddhav Thackeray, all six rebel Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs are likely to merge with Eknath Shinde's Shiv Sena on Monday, giving the faction the crucial two-thirds strength needed to avoid disqualification under the anti-defection law.

The Great IIT Reversal: Why are toppers choosing Civil Engineering over AI?Handcrafted ByOur Editors For years, the IIT dream meant Computer Science. But as AI reshapes careers and India's infrastructure boom gathers pace, some of the country's top JEE Advanced rankers are taking a fresh look at Civil Engineering. Their choices may reflect evolving aspirations among India's future engineers.

12-year-old UP boy watches porn, kidnaps 9-month-old niece, rapes herA nine-month-old girl was allegedly taken from her home in Gorakhpur and assaulted by her 12-year-old maternal uncle. Police said the child is stable in hospital as legal action continues.

He left the IIT path. Today, 1.5 crore people in 169 countries do yoga with himSaurabh Bothra, an IIT-BHU graduate, built a global online yoga platform reaching 15 million learners across 169 countries. The journey from personal practice to daily digital sessions shows how consistency turned yoga into a habit for millions.

Should your past pay cheque define your future salary?You must have gone through rounds of interviews, liked the role, cleared the process, and then the question turns to your previous CTC, and suddenly the new offer falls flat. That is the question at the centre of the salary debate: should your next pay depend on what you earned before, or on what you can do now?

sports.headline: ICC punishes England with 12-point WTC penalty after loss to New Zealand England's World Test Championship (WTC) hopes suffered a major setback after the International Cricket Council (ICC) docked the team 12 points for a slow over-rate during their 253-run defeat to New Zealand in the second Test at The Oval.

A message to selectors? Sooryavanshi's post-match chat after 29-ball 94 goes viral India A vs Sri Lanka A: Following a record-shattering 29-ball 94 against Sri Lanka A, teenage prodigy Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has sent a clear message to senior ODI selectors, reminding his followers of his extensive 50-over pedigree in a viral post-match interview.

Iran leave handwritten note in Los Angeles locker room and it has a plea for peace FIFA World Cup: Iran's players left an emotional handwritten message in their SoFi Stadium locker room on Sunday, thanking Los Angeles for its hospitality and supporters for standing by the team during a challenging World Cup campaign.

Iran played beautiful game under worst conditions: Coach Amir hails Belgium draw Iran coach Amir Ghalenoei hailed his side's performance after a hard-fought 0-0 draw against Belgium in the FIFA World Cup, saying his players produced a "beautiful game" despite battling what he described as the "worst possible conditions" heading into the tournament.

Ignore the numbers? India back clutch Jemimah Rodrigues to defy the critics again Women's T20 World Cup: Amol Muzumdar has given his unflinching backing to the out-of-form Jemimah Rodrigues, labelling her a clutch match-winner as India face a crucial World Cup tightrope following defeat to South Africa.

Egypt end 92-year wait for win at FIFA World Cup as Salah buries New Zealand Mohamed Salah inspired Egypt to a historic 3-1 comeback win over New Zealand on Sunday as the Pharaohs ended a 92-year wait for their first FIFA World Cup victory. Goals from Mostafa Ziko, Salah and Mahmoud Trezeguet secured the landmark result and sent Egypt to the top of Group G.

Cape Verde eye World Cup knockout berth after battling Uruguay draw: Coach Bubista Cape Verde coach Bubista believes his side have earned the right to target a place in the World Cup knockout rounds after battling to a 2-2 draw with Uruguay, a result that kept the debutants firmly in contention for qualification from Group H.

After watching 2022 World Cup from a classroom, Yamal revels in dream debut goal Lamine Yamal marked his first World Cup start with a goal as Spain beat Saudi Arabia 4-0. The strike completed a leap from watching the 2022 tournament in a classroom to shaping Spain's campaign on the pitch.

Vozinha's mum watches on as Cape Verde continue captivating journey Cape Verde's remarkable World Cup story continued on Sunday as they held Uruguay to a 2-2 draw in Miami, with goalkeeper Vozinha's mother, Evora, watching from the stands. After missing her son's historic performance against Spain due to visa issues, Evora was finally able to attend following a successful fundraising effort.

1st WC goal, another point: Cape Verde's fairytale rolls on with Uruguay draw Cape Verde's fairytale got a new chapter as the African side scored their first World Cup goal and secured another point with a 2-2 draw against Uruguay.

Who is Alireza Beiranvand? Iran's wall with two Guinness records stuns Belgium FIFA World Cup 2026: Alireza Beiranvand added his name to the growing list of goalkeeper heroes at FIFA World Cup 2026 with a seven-save masterclass against Belgium. The Iranian shot-stopper, who holds two Guinness World Records, frustrated the Red Devils and kept Iran's knockout hopes alive with a crucial clean sheet.

NZ 1-3 EGY HIGHLIGHTS: Salah inspires Egypt's historic winNew Zealand (NZ) vs Egypt (EGY) HIGHLIGHTS, FIFA World Cup 2026: Egypt's wait for a World Cup victory is finally over. Mohamed Salah was once again the man for the big occasion as the Pharaohs beat New Zealand in a gripping Group G contest, before producing a determined defensive display to see out the closing stages. It is a result that will be celebrated across Egypt, with the national team securing its first-ever win on football's biggest stage.

The Beiranvand wall helps Iran secure vital point against 10-man BelgiumIran held 10-man Belgium to a goalless draw in Los Angeles with Alireza Beiranvand making a series of crucial saves. The point strengthened Iran's knockout hopes and exposed Belgium's wastefulness despite their control of the ball.

Serena Williams set for return to Wimbledon as singles wildcardSerena Williams' remarkable tennis comeback has taken another dramatic turn after the seven-time champion received a Wimbledon singles wildcard. Four years after her last singles appearance at the All England Club, the 23-time Grand Slam winner is set to return to Centre Court once again.

Kohli, Karan Aujla groove on Winning Speech, says song reminds him of his dadVirat Kohli joined Karan Aujla on stage at the One8 event in New Delhi as the singer performed Winning Speech. Kohli later said the song resonates deeply and explain how it reminds him of his father.

Harmanpreet Kaur laments Marizanne Kapp's drop chances after India's costly defeatWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs SA: Marizanne Kapp made India pay for three dropped catches as South Africa beat the reigning ODI world champions by six wickets on Sunday in Manchester.

Lamine Yamal steals the show as Spain roar back to life with Saudi Arabia routFIFA World Cup 2026: Lamine Yamal inspired Spain's 4-0 World Cup win over Saudi Arabia in Atlanta. The result dispelled doubts after the Cape Verde draw and sets up a sterner test against Uruguay.

Sloppy India lose Manchester thriller to South Africa, in danger of early exitWomen's T20 World Cup, IND vs SA: Marizanne Kapp scored an unbeaten 81 as South Africa defeated India by six wickets on Sunday in Manchester. With the defeat, India's upcoming match against Australia now becomes a crucial fixture in the race to secure a place in the semi-finals.

BEL 0-0 IRN Highlights: Iran hold Belgium to stalemateBelgium (BEL) vs Iran (IRN), FIFA World Cup 2026 Highlights: (BEL 0-0 IRN), Iran frustrated Belgium for 90 minutes and were rewarded with a valuable point in Group G. Alireza Beiranvand starred with a string of crucial saves, while Nathan Ngoy's red card compounded a frustrating evening for the Red Devils.

Ben Stokes returns as captain after England refuse to blame him for nightclub rowBen Stokes is back at the helm after the ECB concluded its investigation into the nightclub incident that sidelined him for the second Test. While England's captain was sanctioned for breaching team rules, the board cleared him of any involvement in the violence.

9,230 and out: Virat Kohli firmly rejects talks of reversing Test retirementFormer India captain Virat Kohli has firmly dismissed a return to Test cricket, jokingly stating he would rather undersell his new signature shoe than chase more runs to increase its tribute price tag.

West Indies edge closer to semis after bowlers run riot against Sri LankaWomen's T20 World Cup, SL vs WI: West Indies have continued their impressive run in the tournament, beating Sri Lanka by five wickets in Bristol and strengthening their chances of qualifying for the semis.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi revenge-arc destroys Sri Lanka in tri-series finalIndia A routed Sri Lanka A in the final of the tri-nation series. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yash Thakur starred with the bat and ball respectively as India hammered Sahan Arachchige's side in Dambulla.

IND vs SA Highlights: South Africa beat India by 6 wickets after Kapp heroicsIndia (IND-W) vs South Africa (SA-W) Highlights, Women's T20 World Cup: South Africa beat India by 6 wickets on Sunday at Old Trafford in Manchester to rack-up back-to-back wins. Kapp rocked India with an unbeaten 81 and taking two wickets.

Wahab Riaz blames Pakistan's own mistakes for exit: We beat ourselvesWomen's T20 World Cup, PAK vs BAN: Pakistan coach Wahab Riaz blamed his team's repeated batting collapses for their early exit from the tournament, saying Pakistan had "beaten themselves" rather than being outplayed by opponents.

Where is Mohammed Siraj? Internet abuzz after England ODI squad omissionIndia left Mohammed Siraj out of the ODI squad for England after resting him from the T20Is. With no fresh explanation from the BCCI, the omission has quickly opened questions over workload management, form and selection priorities.

Premier League Buzz: What's happening in the off season?World Cup performances and boardroom churn are accelerating transfer and managerial decisions across the Premier League. From Bouaddi's surge to Rashford's uncertain future, clubs are reshaping squads under pressure.

Ben Stokes to captain England again? Durham star pulled from County clashThe ECB withdrew Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson from their County Championship matches on Sunday, signalling a likely England return for the third Test. The move points to a one-Test suspension over the curfew breach as the enquiry continues.

No place for Yashasvi Jaiswal in ODI squad, Varun Chakravarthy ruled of Ireland T20IsIndia left out Yashasvi Jaiswal for the England ODIs and lost Varun Chakravarthy for the Ireland T20Is. The calls expose fierce competition for places and a squad already being tested by injury.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
